rulings.Case Title: DONALD FARRAR VS. LAWRENCE W WAY MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 1. ATTORNEY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL AS ATTORNEY OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFFS DONALD FARRAR AND PRISCILLA FARRAR. Hearing required. The counsel seeking to withdraw must bring courtesy copies of the moving papers to the hearing and a proposed order on the Judicial Council form. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS ASSOCIATION INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. RED ZONE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DBA RED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Apporval Of Final Account, Final Report, Apporving Receiver'S Fees And Costs, Discharging Receiver And Exonerating Surety
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ASSOCIATION INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN's Motion For Apporval Of Final Account, Final Report, Approving Receiver'S Fees And Costs, Discharging Receiver And Exonerating Surety. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Amend X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 2. CROSS COMPLAINANT DEMAS YAN'S Motion To Amend X-Complaint. Denied. Mr. Yan does not provide good cause for seeking to file a cross-complaint shortly before trial. Cross-defendants would suffer prejudice if the motion was granted. Moreover, some or all of the proposed cross-claims appear to be time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Nick Volente, Kamran Ardebilchi And Jahangir Ardebilchi'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Site Inspection,And Deposition From Plaintiff Paul Gerrard And Request For Tertminating Sanctions (Continued From 5/11/12)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 3. Defendants Nick Volente, Kamran Ardebilchi And Jahangir Ardebilchi'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Site Inspection,And Deposition From Plaintiff Paul Gerrard And Request For Tertminating Sanctions (Continued From 5/11/12). The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Jude Paul Renne as follows: Granted. Plaintiff failed to comply with the July 7, 2011 and April 18, 2012 Orders requiring Plaintiff to provide verifications and supplemental responses to form interrogatories. Plaintiff also failed to comply with the July 7, 2011 and April 18, 2012 Court Order requiring Plaintiff to make himself available for deposition. Plaintiff also failed to comply with the July 7, 2011 and April 18, 2012 Court Order requiring Plaintiff to make his unit at the Sheldon Hotel available for inspection. The Complaint of Plaintiff Paul Garrard in this matter is dismissed in its entirety with prejudice. Pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure section 581d, Judgment is entered in favor of Defendants Kamran Ardeilbchi, Jahangir Ardebilchi and Nick Volente and against Plaintiff Paul Garrard. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SERRAMONTE RIDGE LLC VS. JEFFERSON UNION HIGH SCHOOL DISTRICT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Serramonte Ridge Llc'S Notice Of Motion Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 6. Petitioner Serramonte Ridge Llc'S Notice Of Motion Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. Granted per Code of Civil Procedure sections 1286.2(a)(3), (4), and (5). On the dispositive issue of whether there was an oral agreement that Mr. Carneghi could conduct whatever post-hearing investigation into the facts that he deemed necessary without need for a further hearing, the court finds that no such agreement was knowingly made by petitioner. In so finding, the court does not impute bad faith or dishonesty on the part of Mr. Carneghi or anyone else who may have believed that there was such an agreement. The interests of justice require that the determination of the rent be made by an entirely new panel of three appraisers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 12. DEFENDANTS DIEGO RIOS, DEANNA CRESPO AND RACHIALLE FRANKLIN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. Even if plaintiff's unfiled application for a default judgment were construed as an opposition to this motion, that opposition lacks merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Restricting Depositions And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Attorney Bradley White
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN, PHIL FOSTER, PAUL TOUR-SARKISSIAN, TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES' Motion For Protective Order Restricting Depositions And Imposing Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Attorney Bradley White. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VP & RB CORPORATION VS. MAIL BOXES ETC., INC., et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN DIEGO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer; Memo; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANT MAIL BOXES ETC., INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer. Denied. Agreements fixing venue are void as a matter of public policy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL SANCHEZ et al VS. NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS DBA S.F. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 31, 2012, Line 16. DEFENDANTS NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS DBA S.F., DON BATTAGLIA AND NEW SAN FRANCISCO BAIT DISTRIBUTORS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained as to all claims with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Miguel Sanchez needs to clarify precisely the claims he is alleging, the relief he is seeking and the statutory basis for suing on behalf of others. The amended complaint also needs to allege additional ultimate facts regarding most of the claims, but in particular the wrongful discharge, negligent infliction and retaliation claims. . Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
